title: Exponential growth vs. decay
description: Identify whether an exponential functions represents growth or decay.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Does the function model exponential growth or decay?**

$f(t)=\dfrac14\cdot4^t$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This table summarizes the differences between exponential growth and decay:

 | Direction | Shape | Common ratio
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
**Growth** | Increasing | [[☃ image 1]] | Greater than $1$
**Decay** | Decreasing | [[☃ image 2]] | Less than $1$
The common ratio for this function is $4$, which is greater than $1$. So each time $t$ increases, the value of $f(t)$ grows by a factor of $4$.
The function models exponential growth.

choices.content: Growth
Decay

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the shape of the graph of the function?**

$g(x)=\dfrac32\cdot\left(\dfrac23\right)^x$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This table summarizes the differences between exponential growth and decay:

 | Direction | Shape | Common ratio
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
**Growth** | Increasing | [[☃ image 1]] | Greater than $1$
**Decay** | Decreasing | [[☃ image 2]] | Less than $1$

The common ratio for this function is $\dfrac{2}{3}$, which is less than $1$. So each time $x$ increases, the value of $g(x)$ decays by a factor of $\dfrac{2}{3}$.

The graph should show a decreasing function.

This is the shape of the graph of the function:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A curve that increases slowly at first, then gradually increases more rapidly.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ddfdd574fea63c8a962d9ba26cf27f83ab3f2903)
![A curve that decreases rapidly at first, then gradually decreases more slowly.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ca1686e518c49fbc6d441662c385f1bba474661e)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the shape of the graph of the function?**

$h(t)=0.6\cdot 3.2^t$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This table summarizes the differences between exponential growth and decay:

 | Direction | Shape | Common ratio
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
**Growth** | Increasing | ![The function appears as a line sloping uphill from left to right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4cca632f02ce4a7fb8d02c2e7b4dc5f7ff85507b) | Greater than $1$
**Decay** | Decreasing | ![The function appears as a line sloping downwards from left to right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e5da332a3672bae24f9eb70a9a3be7631e0c3b27) | Less than $1$

The common ratio for this function is $3.2$, which is greater than $1$. So each time $t$ increases, the value of $h(t)$ grows by a factor of $3.2$.

The graph should show an increasing function.

This is the shape of the graph of the function:

![The function appears as a line sloping uphill from left to right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ddfdd574fea63c8a962d9ba26cf27f83ab3f2903)

choices.content: ![The function appears as a line sloping uphill from left to right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ddfdd574fea63c8a962d9ba26cf27f83ab3f2903)
![The function appears as a line sloping downwards from left to right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ca1686e518c49fbc6d441662c385f1bba474661e)
